# ARC-QUIC-CRT-0036 - QUIC CRT client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt architecture

## Purpose

Describe how the managed client/runtime path admits the first bounded 0-RTT packet-emission attempt from the dormant detached carrier without widening 0-RTT receive handling or anti-replay.

## Scope

This slice is limited to the first client-side 0-RTT packet-emission attempt. It does not add 0-RTT receive handling, anti-replay enforcement, key update, transfer, retry, or any public API widening.

## Requirements Satisfied

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0141

## Design Summary

The managed client/runtime path will keep the dormant detached carrier and the readiness observation as the precondition for a first client-side 0-RTT send attempt. When that precondition is satisfied, the runtime will derive a distinct 0-RTT packet-protection package from the carrier and the live transport state, then feed the protected packet into the existing connection-owned send path. The current 1-RTT send path remains unchanged, and the slice stops at the first send attempt rather than expanding into receive handling or replay control.

## Key Components

- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnection.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicClientConnectionHost.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicConnectionRuntime.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicDetachedResumptionTicketSnapshot.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicHandshakeFlowCoordinator.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsKeySchedule.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsPacketProtectionMaterial.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransport.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTlsTransportBridgeDriver.cs
- src/Incursa.Quic/QuicTransportTlsBridgeState.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/QuicResumptionClientHelloTestSupport.cs
- tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/CRT/REQ-QUIC-CRT-0141.cs

## Data and State Considerations

The detached carrier already holds the resumption credential material and the early-data prerequisite material captured by the earlier slices. The new 0-RTT attempt boundary may need a separate internal protection package or encryption-level marker, but it must stay distinct from the owned 1-RTT material and from the public API surface. Missing or invalid prerequisite material must leave the send gate closed.

## Edge Cases and Constraints

- The slice stays client-role only and managed client/runtime only.
- Missing dormant carrier material or a failed 0-RTT derivation must keep the send gate closed.
- The 0-RTT packet-protection package must remain distinct from the current 1-RTT package and must not alias its state.
- The first send attempt must not imply 0-RTT receive support, server admission, anti-replay, or key-update support.
- The accepted abbreviated resumption boundary from `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0137` and the readiness observation from `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0140` remain unchanged.

## Alternatives Considered

- Add 0-RTT receive handling in the same slice. Rejected because the first honest send-attempt boundary is already large enough and receive/replay is a separate family.
- Keep the family as readiness only. Rejected because the repo already has the readiness observation and now needs a real send-attempt boundary.
- Promote the attempt floor into public API. Rejected because the slice must remain internal and managed-client only.

## Risks

- If the 0-RTT packet-protection package reuses the 1-RTT material or API shape, the slice will overclaim supported 1-RTT behavior instead of a distinct early-data attempt.
- If the send gate leaks into public API, the attempt floor will widen the promise instead of remaining an internal client/runtime decision.
- If receive handling or anti-replay is folded into the same slice, the family becomes too large to prove honestly in one turn.
- If fallback on missing prerequisites is not clean, the slice could leave the runtime looking ready when it is not.
